nodes.label: stream | flow | forest
nodes.sentence: Near a forest in a distant land, a clear stream flowed beside a mill, and nearby stood the miller’s house.

nodes.label: miller | have | daughter
nodes.sentence: The miller had a very beautiful daughter, who was also clever and sharp.

nodes.label: miller | tell | king
nodes.sentence: Proud of her, the miller told the king, who often hunted in the woods, that his daughter could spin straw into gold.

nodes.label: king | become | ∅
nodes.sentence: The king, who loved money, became greedy and ordered the girl to be brought before him.

nodes.label: he | take | she
nodes.sentence: He took her to a room filled with straw, gave her a spinning wheel, and commanded, “Spin all this straw into gold by morning, or your life is forfeit.”

nodes.label: girl | protest | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The poor girl protested that this was only her father’s boast and she could not do such a thing, but the door was locked, and she was left alone.

Sitting down in a corner, she cried for her hard fate.

nodes.label: door | open | ∅
nodes.sentence: Suddenly, the door opened, and a strange little man hobbled in.

nodes.label: you | cry | ∅
nodes.sentence: Why are you crying?”

nodes.label: he | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: he asked.

nodes.label: she | tell | he
nodes.sentence: She told him of her impossible task.

nodes.label: he | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: “What will you give me to spin it into gold for you?” he asked.

nodes.label: she | offer | necklace
nodes.sentence: She offered her necklace, and he agreed.

nodes.label: he | whistle | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Sitting at the wheel, he whistled and sang:

“Round about, round about, 
Look and see!

nodes.label: ∅ | reel | gold
nodes.sentence:
Reel and spin, 
Straw to gold!”

nodes.label: wheel | turn | ∅
nodes.sentence: The wheel turned quickly, and by morning all the straw was spun into gold.

nodes.label: he | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the king saw the gold, he was amazed and pleased, but his greed grew stronger.

nodes.label: he | lock | daughter
nodes.sentence: He locked the miller’s daughter in a bigger room filled with more straw and ordered her to spin it all into gold by morning.

nodes.label: she | weep | ∅
nodes.sentence: Again, she wept not knowing what to do.

nodes.label: man | appear | ∅
nodes.sentence: The little man appeared and asked, “What will you give me now?”

nodes.label: she | offer | ring
nodes.sentence: She offered her ring.

nodes.label: he | take | it
nodes.sentence: He took it and began to spin, singing his song, until all the straw was gold before dawn.

nodes.label: king | overjoy | ∅
nodes.sentence: The king was overjoyed but still desired more.

nodes.label: he | lead | daughter
nodes.sentence: He led the miller’s daughter to an even larger pile of straw and declared that if she spun it all by morning, she could become his queen.

nodes.label: dwarf | appear | ∅
nodes.sentence: Alone once more, the dwarf appeared and asked what she would give him this time.

nodes.label: she | have | nothing
nodes.sentence: She had nothing left but agreed to give him the first child she would bear as queen.

nodes.label: man | agree | ∅
nodes.sentence: The little man agreed, spun the straw to gold again, and the king, seeing the gold, married her, making her queen.

nodes.label: queen | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: When her first child was born, the queen was happy and forgot the dwarf and their promise.

nodes.label: man | appear | room
nodes.sentence: One day, the little man appeared in her room while she played with her baby and reminded her of the bargain.

nodes.label: she | heartbroken | ∅
nodes.sentence: She was heartbroken and offered him all her kingdom’s wealth to let her keep the child, but he refused.

nodes.label: i | give | day
nodes.sentence: Finally, moved by her tears, he said, “I will give you three days.

nodes.label: you | keep | baby
nodes.sentence: If by then you can guess my name, you may keep your baby.”

nodes.label: queen | stay | ∅
nodes.sentence: The queen stayed awake all night thinking of every name she knew and sent messengers far and wide to find unusual names.

nodes.label: she | guess | name
nodes.sentence: On the first day, when the dwarf returned, she guessed many names—Timothy, Ichabod, Benjamin, Jeremiah—but he denied each one.

nodes.label: she | try | name
nodes.sentence: On the second day, she tried funny names—Bandy-legs, Hunchback, Crook-shanks—but he rejected those too.

nodes.label: messenger | return | day
nodes.sentence: On the third day, a messenger returned with news.

nodes.label: he | spot | hut
nodes.sentence: While climbing a hill through the forest where fox and hare said goodnight, he had spotted a small hut.

nodes.label: ∅ | stand | fire
nodes.sentence:
By the fire stood a funny little dwarf dancing on one leg, singing:

“Merrily I feast and bake,

nodes.label: i | brew | ∅
nodes.sentence: Today I’ll brew and make;

nodes.label: lady | guess | name
nodes.sentence:
Next day a stranger I will bring— 
Little does my lady guess my name: 
Rumpelstiltskin!”

nodes.label: queen | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Hearing this, the queen was joyful.

nodes.label: she | gather | court
nodes.sentence: When the little man came again, she gathered her court around her throne, the nurse beside her holding the baby as if ready to give him up.

nodes.label: child | be | his
nodes.sentence: The dwarf grinned, certain the child was his.

nodes.label: he | demand | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Tell me my name,” he demanded.

nodes.label: it | be | john
nodes.sentence: “Is it John?”

nodes.label: she | guess | ∅
nodes.sentence: she guessed.

nodes.label: he | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: “No,” he said.

nodes.label: it | be | rumpelstiltskin
nodes.sentence: “Is it Rumpelstiltskin?”

nodes.label: she | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence: she asked slyly.

nodes.label: man | shout | ∅
nodes.sentence: The little man shouted, “A witch told you that!

nodes.label: witch | tell | that
nodes.sentence: A witch told you that!”

nodes.label: he | stamp | foot
nodes.sentence: In a furious rage, he stamped his right foot so hard into the floor that he had to grab it with both hands to pull it out.

nodes.label: he | run | ∅
nodes.sentence: Then he ran off as fast as he could.

nodes.label: baby | coo | ∅
nodes.sentence: The nurse laughed, the baby cooed, and the whole court jeered at him for all his trouble, crying out, “Good morning, Mr. Rumpelstiltskin, and happy feasting!”
